Robot mode operations on user panes require --all flag but this is not documented.

```
By default, only agent panes (2+) are included.
```

2. --all flag behavior documented
3. Examples show usage with and without --all
4. Robot mode help text includes note
5.  {"id":"bd-3iglm","title":"Add ntm assign --retry for retrying failed assignments","description":"## Overview
Retry failed assignments with a single command.

## Use Cases
1. Agent crashed mid-task, want to retry on same or different agent
2. Agent gave up due to transient issue
3. Batch retry all failures after investigation

## Usage
```bash
# Retry specific bead
ntm assign myproject --retry=bd-xyz

# Retry specific bead to specific agent
ntm assign myproject --retry=bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Retry all failed assignments
ntm assign myproject --retry-failed

# Retry all failed for a specific agent type
ntm assign myproject --retry-failed --agent=codex

# Retry with different strategy (reassign to best agent)
ntm assign myproject --retry-failed --strategy=quality
```

## How It Works
1. Find failed assignments matching filter
2. Clear the failed assignment (reset status)
3. If --to-pane: Assign to specific pane
4. Else: Use strategy to pick best agent
5. Create new assignment, send prompt
6. Return results

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "retry",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "retried": [
      {
        "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
        "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
        "pane": 4,
        "agent_type": "claude",
        "agent_name": "RedStone",
        "status": "assigned",
        "prompt_sent": true,
        "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
        "previous_pane": 3,
        "previous_agent": "BlueLake",
        "previous_fail_reason": "agent crashed",
        "retry_count": 1
      }
    ],
    "skipped": [
      {
        "bead_id": "bd-abc",
        "reason": "no idle agents available"
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "total_failed": 5,
      "retried_count": 4,
      "skipped_count": 1
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Retry vs Reassign
- **--retry**: For failed assignments only
- **--reassign**: For any assignment (working, assigned, failed)

## Error Handling
- No failed assignments: Return success with empty retried array
- No idle agents: Add to skipped with reason
- Bead no longer exists: Add to skipped with reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Retry single bead by ID
- [ ] Retry to specific pane
- [ ] Retry all failed (--retry-failed)
- [ ] Filter by agent type
- [ ] Uses strategy for new assignment
- [ ] Preserves assignment history (previous_* fields)
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Includes retry_count

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Retry logic
- Unit test: Filter matching
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Retry workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:54:48.388293630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:40:02.793664994Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:40:02.793619128Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iglm","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3iglm","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ihtb","title":"Unit Tests: PromptInjector timing and formatting","description":"## Purpose
Unit tests for PromptInjector covering prompt formatting, staggered delivery, and agent-specific quirks.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/prompt_injector_test.go
package swarm

func TestPromptInjector_FormatPrompt(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        agentType string
        prompt    string
        expected  string
    }{
        {
            name:      "codex_double_enter",
            agentType: "cod",
            prompt:    "Run the tests",
            expected:  "\n\nRun the tests\n",
        },
        {
            name:      "claude_normal",
            agentType: "cc",
            prompt:    "Read AGENTS.md",
            expected:  "Read AGENTS.md\n",
        },
        {
            name:      "gemini_normal",
            agentType: "gmi",
            prompt:    "Fix the bug",
            expected:  "Fix the bug\n",
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            injector := NewPromptInjector(nil, nil)
            result := injector.formatPrompt(tt.agentType, tt.prompt)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Agent: %s, Input: %q", tt.agentType, tt.prompt)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Output: %q", result)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
        })
    }
}

func TestPromptInjector_StaggeredDelivery(t *testing.T) {
    mockPaneSpawner := &MockPaneSpawner{}
    tracker := ratelimit.NewRateLimitTracker("")
    
    injector := NewPromptInjector(mockPaneSpawner, tracker)
    injector.baseDelay = 100 * time.Millisecond
    
    panes := []string{"session:1.0", "session:1.1", "session:1.2"}
    prompt := "Start work"
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Starting staggered delivery to 3 panes")
    
    start := time.Now()
    var deliveryTimes []time.Duration
    
    for _, pane := range panes {
        t.Logf("[TEST] Injecting to %s", pane)
        _, err := injector.InjectPrompt(pane, "cc", prompt)
        require.NoError(t, err)
        deliveryTimes = append(deliveryTimes, time.Since(start))
    }
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Delivery times: %v", deliveryTimes)
    
    // Verify staggering
    for i := 1; i < len(deliveryTimes); i++ {
        gap := deliveryTimes[i] - deliveryTimes[i-1]
        t.Logf("[TEST] Gap %d->%d: %v", i-1, i, gap)
        assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, gap, 90*time.Millisecond)
    }
}

func TestPromptInjector_ReviewModePrompts(t *testing.T) {
    injector := NewPromptInjector(nil, nil)
    
    prompt := injector.getReviewModePrompt("gmi")
    t.Logf("[TEST] Review mode prompt for gmi: %s", prompt[:50])
    
    assert.Contains(t, prompt, "review")
    assert.Contains(t, prompt, "code quality")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test prompt formatting for all agent types
- [ ] Test Codex double-Enter quirk handling
- [ ] Test staggered delivery timing
- [ ] Test rate limit integration
- [ ] Test review-mode prompts
- [ ] Detailed test logging with [TEST] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:32:50.813292584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:12:06.533845924Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:12:06.533768718Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ihtb","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3iomn","title":"Task: REST output tooling + palette/history endpoints","description":"## Scope\nExpose output tooling plus palette/history endpoints via REST.\n\n## Endpoints\n- Output: /output/copy, /output/save, /output/grep, /output/extract, /output/diff, /output/changes, /output/conflicts, /output/summary, /output/watch.\n- Palette: /palette, /palette/run, /palette/pin, /palette/favorite.\n- History: /history, /history/{id}, /history/clear, /history/stats, /history/export, /history/prune, /history/replay.\n\n## Logging\n- Log session/pane IDs and output action type.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: request validation + error codes.\n- Integration: copy/save/extract and verify artifacts.\n- Parity: REST outputs match CLI for deterministic commands.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Endpoints mirror CLI functionality and output envelopes.\n- History operations preserve existing storage semantics.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:50:03.180532360Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:00:02.623567496Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:00:02.623549032Z","close_reason":"Added REST endpoints for output (tail, diff, files, summary), palette, history, route, and wait APIs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iomn","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3iomn","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ivl8","title":"Task: Add process_state field from /proc","description":"## What

Add `process_state` field showing the kernel-reported state of the agent process.

## Why

A process can be "running" but actually in various states:
- **R (Running)**: Actually executing
- **S (Sleeping)**: Waiting for I/O (normal for agents)
- **D (Disk sleep)**: Uninterruptible I/O wait (may indicate problem)
- **Z (Zombie)**: Exited but not reaped (definitely problem)
- **T (Stopped)**: Suspended (unusual)

Zombie processes especially need different handling than running ones.

## Implementation

```go
func getProcessState(pid int) (string, error) {
    data, err := os.ReadFile(fmt.Sprintf("/proc/%d/status", pid))
    if err != nil {
        return "unknown", err
    }
    
    for _, line := range strings.Split(string(data), "\n") {
        if strings.HasPrefix(line, "State:") {
            // Format: "State:  S (sleeping)"
            parts := strings.Fields(line)
            if len(parts) >= 2 {
                return parts[1], nil  // Just the letter
            }
        }
    }
    return "unknown", nil
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "child_pid": 12346,
    "process_state": "S",
    "process_state_name": "sleeping"  // Human readable
  }]
}
```

## State Mapping

| Code | Name | Meaning | Action |
|------|------|---------|--------|
| R | running | CPU bound | Normal |
| S | sleeping | I/O wait | Normal |
| D | disk_sleep | Uninterruptible | May be stuck |
| Z | zombie | Dead but not reaped | Needs cleanup |
| T | stopped | Suspended | Unusual |
| X | dead | Should not see | Error state |

## Handling No Process

If child_pid is null or process doesn't exist, return:
```json
{
  "process_state": null,
  "process_state_name": "not_found"
}
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Mock /proc reads for each state
- Integration test: Verify against actual processes","notes":"Feature already implemented in status_enrich.go. getProcessState function reads /proc/<pid>/status and enrichAgentStatus populates ProcessState and ProcessStateName fields.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:19:04.046091988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:39:47.802323692Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:39:47.802229153Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ivl8","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ixg4","title":"CLI: Add --auto-rotate-accounts flag","description":"## Purpose
Implement --panes-per-session flag to set maximum panes per tmux session, overriding the calculated default.

## Implementation Details

```go
// Flag definition
var panesPerSession int

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().IntVar(&panesPerSession, "panes-per-session", 0,
        "Max panes per session (0 = auto-calculate from total agents)")
}

// Validation and application
func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    logger := slog.Default()
    
    // Validate panes-per-session if provided
    if panesPerSession < 0 {
        return fmt.Errorf("--panes-per-session cannot be negative, got %d", panesPerSession)
    }
    if panesPerSession > 20 {
        logger.Warn("high panes-per-session may cause crowded sessions",
            "value", panesPerSession,
            "recommended_max", 20)
    }
    
    config := loadSwarmConfig(configPath)
    
    if panesPerSession > 0 {
        // Override auto-calculation
        config.PanesPerSession = panesPerSession
        logger.Info("panes-per-session override",
            "value", panesPerSession,
            "mode", "manual")
    } else {
        // Auto-calculate: will be set during plan generation
        logger.Info("panes-per-session",
            "mode", "auto-calculate")
    }
    
    // ... continue with plan generation ...
}

// Auto-calculation formula (in allocation.go):
// panesPerSession = ceil(maxAgentsOfAnyType / sessionsPerType)
//
// Example:
//   Total CC: 12, Total Cod: 8, Total Gmi: 6
//   Max: 12
//   Sessions per type: 3
//   Auto panes: ceil(12/3) = 4 panes per session
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log whether value is manual or auto-calculated
- Warn for high values (>20)
- Log final panes-per-session in plan output

## Interaction with --sessions-per-type
- If both are set, panes-per-session takes precedence
- More sessions + fewer panes = better for monitoring
- Fewer sessions + more panes = simpler management

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default 0 (auto-calculate)
- [ ] Manual override when > 0
- [ ] Cannot be negative
- [ ] Warning for values > 20
- [ ] Clear logging of calculation mode
- [ ] Documented interaction with sessions-per-type","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:17.486921029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T08:06:21.917061478Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ixg4","depends_on_id":"bd-1cg8z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ixg4","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3uda9","title":"Integration: Wire SwarmOrchestrator into existing health monitoring","description":"## Purpose
Integrate AllocationCalculator with SessionOrchestrator to execute the generated SwarmPlan, creating tmux sessions and launching agents.

## Implementation Details

```go
// SwarmExecutor orchestrates the complete swarm launch sequence
type SwarmExecutor struct {
    calculator   *AllocationCalculator
    orchestrator *session.SessionOrchestrator
    launcher     *PaneLauncher
    injector     *PromptInjector
    logger       *slog.Logger
}

// Execute runs the complete swarm launch workflow
func (e *SwarmExecutor) Execute(ctx context.Context, plan *SwarmPlan) error {
    e.logger.Info("starting swarm execution",
        "total_agents", plan.TotalAgents,
        "sessions", len(plan.Sessions),
        "scan_dir", plan.ScanDir)
    
    // Phase 1: Create all tmux sessions with panes
    e.logger.Info("phase 1: creating tmux sessions")
    for _, sessionSpec := range plan.Sessions {
        if err := e.createSession(ctx, sessionSpec); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("create session %s: %w", sessionSpec.Name, err)
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 2: Launch agents in all panes
    e.logger.Info("phase 2: launching agents")
    for _, sessionSpec := range plan.Sessions {
        for _, paneSpec := range sessionSpec.Panes {
            if err := e.launcher.LaunchAgentInPane(ctx, sessionSpec.Name, paneSpec); err != nil {
                e.logger.Error("agent launch failed",
                    "session", sessionSpec.Name,
                    "pane", paneSpec.Index,
                    "error", err)
                // Continue with other panes - partial success allowed
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 3: Inject initial prompts (if configured)
    if plan.InitialPrompt != "" {
        e.logger.Info("phase 3: injecting prompts")
        if err := e.injectPrompts(ctx, plan); err != nil {
            e.logger.Warn("prompt injection had errors", "error", err)
        }
    }
    
    e.logger.Info("swarm execution complete",
        "sessions_created", len(plan.Sessions),
        "total_agents", plan.TotalAgents)
    
    return nil
}

func (e *SwarmExecutor) createSession(ctx context.Context, spec SessionSpec) error {
    e.logger.Info("creating session",
        "name", spec.Name,
        "agent_type", spec.AgentType,
        "pane_count", spec.PaneCount)
    
    // Use SessionOrchestrator to create session with required panes
    if err := e.orchestrator.CreateSession(spec.Name, spec.PaneCount); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("orchestrator create: %w", err)
    }
    
    e.logger.Debug("session created successfully", "name", spec.Name)
    return nil
}
```

## Integration Points
- AllocationCalculator.GenerateSwarmPlan() -> SwarmPlan
- SessionOrchestrator.CreateSession() for tmux operations
- PaneLauncher.LaunchAgentInPane() for agent startup
- PromptInjector.Inject() for marching orders

## Logging Requirements
- Log phase transitions clearly
- Log session/pane counts at each phase
- Log individual failures but continue execution
- Summary log at completion

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Three-phase execution (sessions, agents, prompts)
- [ ] Uses existing SessionOrchestrator
- [ ] Partial success handling (continue on pane failure)
- [ ] Context cancellation support
- [ ] Phase-based structured logging
- [ ] Error aggregation for summary","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:31.785460344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T16:52:29.089339808Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uda9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0eb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3uda9","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3uda9","depends_on_id":"bd-kjihq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uj4","title":"Task: Install git hooks","description":"## Purpose
Install git hooks for beads sync and Agent Mail guard during project initialization.

## Hooks to Install
1. pre-commit: Run br sync --flush-only to export beads changes
2. post-checkout: Warn about uncommitted bead changes
3. Agent Mail guard: Block commits that conflict with file reservations

## Scope
- Create .git/hooks/ scripts if .git exists
- Make hooks executable (chmod +x)
- Support --no-hooks flag to skip
- Do not overwrite existing hooks (append or warn)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pre-commit hook runs br sync --flush-only before commit
- Hooks are executable
- Existing hooks not overwritten
- Works with or without Agent Mail

## Technical Notes
Check if .git directory exists before attempting
Use embedded hook templates","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:30:30.418272599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T08:54:06.382217879Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T08:54:06.382168165Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uj4","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-3ury8","title":"Task: E2E Tests: JFP robot wrapper","description":"## Overview
End-to-end tests for JFP robot wrapper commands with real CLI if available.

## Scenarios
1. `--robot-jfp-list` returns JSON and includes known prompt IDs
2. `--robot-jfp-search` returns hits for a common query
3. `--robot-jfp-show` returns full prompt content
4. `--robot-jfp-install` (dry-run or temp project) installs a skill
5. Missing jfp returns DEPENDENCY_MISSING with hint

## Logging
Use log/slog:

+{"id":"bd-3ury8","title":"Task: E2E Tests: JFP robot wrapper","description":"## Overview
End-to-end tests for JFP robot wrapper commands with real CLI if available.

## Scenarios
1. `--robot-jfp-list` returns JSON and includes known prompt IDs
2. `--robot-jfp-search` returns hits for a common query
3. `--robot-jfp-show` returns full prompt content
4. `--robot-jfp-install` (dry-run or temp project) installs a skill
5. Missing jfp returns DEPENDENCY_MISSING with hint

- [E2E-JFP] error=<err> hint=<hint>

 {"id":"bd-3ut5","title":"Feature: Extend existing notifications with webhook events","description":"## Overview
NTM already supports webhook notifications. This task **extends** the existing notification system with additional event types (not a new webhook subsystem).

## Scope
- Add event types for: agent-started, bead-assigned, bead-completed, error
- Ensure webhook payload includes session, pane, agent type, and bead ID when applicable
- Reuse existing notifications config + templates

## Tests
- Unit: template rendering for new event fields
- E2E: trigger events and assert webhook payloads (detailed logging)

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-WEBHOOK] event=%s session=%s payload_bytes=%d", …)

## Non-Goals
- No separate webhook framework
- No new config files

## Acceptance Criteria
- New events appear in existing notifications pipeline
- Webhook payloads include relevant context fields
- Robot mode outputs can reference notification event IDs when needed
- Unit + E2E tests cover new event types and payload rendering","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:40.468678597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:35:58.814000946Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3uydb","title":"Feature: Mode Output Caching","description":"## Summary
Cache mode outputs when context hasn't changed so users can re-run synthesis or compare strategies without re-running expensive analysis.

## Rationale
Caching speeds experimentation and reduces cost when only synthesis strategy changes.

## Implementation
- Cache key includes ContextPack hash + mode_id + mode_version + relevant config hashes (preamble/strategy/model).
- Store serialized ModeOutput in `.ntm/ensemble-cache/` with TTL (default 24h, configurable).
- Invalidate on context change, mode version change, explicit `--no-cache`, or TTL expiry.
- CLI flags: `--use-cache` (default) and `--no-cache`.
- Status output shows hit/miss/invalidations and reason codes.

## Logging
- Log cache hit/miss + invalidation reason (context_hash_changed, ttl_expired, version_bump).

## Tests
- Unit: cache key determinism, TTL expiry, invalidation reasons
- Integration: cache survives restart
- E2E: rerun uses cache; `--no-cache` forces refresh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cache key deterministically derived
- [ ] Cached outputs restored correctly
- [ ] Invalidations occur for each trigger
- [ ] CLI flags override cache behavior
- [ ] Logs and status show hit/miss metrics

## Dependencies
- Requires ModeOutput schema (bd-1wnxl)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:06:03.033064607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:54:50.137419358Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uydb","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3uydb","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v03r","title":"CAAM: Add dashboard widget for account status","description":"Add dashboard widget showing current account and usage per provider.

## Design

### Widget Location
Side panel or bottom section of dashboard.

### Visual Layout
```
┌─ Accounts ────────────────────┐
│ Claude:  acct1@x.com  [███░░] │
│ OpenAI:  acct2@y.com  [████░] │  <- 80% alert (yellow)
│ Gemini:  acct3@z.com  [█░░░░] │
└───────────────────────────────┘
```

### Features
- Progress bar showing usage/limit
- Color coding: green (<60%), yellow (60-80%), red (>80%)
- Click to expand: show all accounts, switch option
- Cooldown indicator for rate-limited accounts

## Implementation

### Model
```go
type AccountsWidget struct {
    caamManager *caam.Manager
    accounts    []AccountStatus
    width       int
    height      int
}

type AccountStatus struct {
    Provider     string
    Email        string
    UsagePercent int
    InCooldown   bool
    CooldownEnd  time.Time
}
```

### View
Use lipgloss for progress bars and colors:
```go
func (w AccountsWidget) View() string {
    // Render each provider row
    // Include progress bar with color
    // Show alert icon if >80%
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/tui/widgets/accounts.go
- Update: internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go

## Refresh
Poll caam status every 30 seconds (or on account switch event).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:53:15.682385815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:51.711753399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:51.711702493Z","close_reason":"Implemented accounts.go panel with CAAM status display","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-3b52y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
